title: fix for Florence-2 space
body:
previously Florence2 had flash attention as a requirement due to an error in *transformers* (issue with packages Forge uses rather than in Forge itself) (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/Florence-2-base/discussions/4#6673ffb9436907f83a8aaf2d); the workaround was to patch the imports to remove it (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/phi-1_5/discussions/72#65a0339f64520347aa3d703c).
At some point, an update has made this patch unnecessary. And turned it into an error.

This PR simply removes the patch.

Now the Space loads again; tested a couple of functions as working correctly too.

#2299

step_plan.summary: Remove the Florence-2-specific monkeypatch that stripped 'flash_attn' from transformers' dynamic imports by deleting fixed_get_imports, removing the unittest.mock.patch usage and related imports, and restoring the normal model-loading block so the Space loads without error.

steps.operation: Remove the Florence-2-specific monkeypatch (fixed_get_imports) and its usage, clean up related imports, and unindent the model-loading block
steps.reason: Upstream transformers no longer requires the workaround that stripped 'flash_attn' from dynamic imports; the monkeypatch now causes import errors. Removing the helper function fixed_get_imports, the with patch(...) context that replaced transformers.dynamic_module_utils.get_imports, and the unused imports eliminates the harmful patch and restores normal model loading. Unindenting the model-loading block ensures models are loaded inside the existing spaces.capture_gpu_object() context without the temporary patch.
steps.side_effects: Changes model-loading behavior for the Florence-2 Space back to the default transformers import resolution. Tests to run: start the Space locally and load microsoft/Florence-2-large, then run example tasks (Caption, OD, OCR) to verify model loads and inference works; run unit/integration tests that touch this Space. Potential impacts: If users run on older transformers versions that still exhibited the original bug, they may encounter the original import issue again (consider documenting or adding a narrowly-scoped conditional workaround in a future PR if needed). No public API changes expected beyond the Space behavior; removing the monkeypatch may affect any code that relied on the previous patched behavior. Also ensure no linter/unused-import warnings remain after removal.
